title: Lucid Profitability Timeline and Cash Burn Analysis
text: Lucid (LCID) Profitability Timeline and Cash Burn Analysis. Cash Burn. Lucid is burning approximately $2.9 billion in cash annually. The $4.6 billion in total liquidity at year-end 2025, plus the $1.05 billion raised in April 2026, gives approximately $5.65 billion in total resources—enough for less than two years at current burn if no revenue growth occurs. Profitability Timeline. Lucid has yet to provide a specific timeline for profitability despite ongoing operational improvements. 2025 Performance. The Company delivered 15,841 vehicles in 2025. It has never made money and has spent two years consistently missing production targets. 2026 Outlook. Lucid announced its 2026 annual production guidance of 25,000–27,000 vehicles, representing significant growth. The company's focus remains on operational and financial discipline, sustainable growth, and continued progress toward profitability. Key Risk Factor. Lucid is losing money on every vehicle it sells. Scaling production and sales without a path to unit economics is a recipe for eventual insolvency. The company's profitability depends heavily on success with a planned mid-size SUV launch in 2026.
